summary: Fix snap Polygon2D editor

description.text:
* *fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110966*

**Issue Description**

When we drag a vertex of a Polygon from polygon editor.

- Pressed mouse left key, record mouse position as `drag_from`
- Drag to somewhere and release mouse, get mouse position as `drag_to`, depending on `grid snap` configuration, we will calculate with `snap_point()` to let mouse position snap to the grid
- The problem is we used `drag_to - drag_from` as distance to update points of polygon, that caused very hard to drag  a vertex to snap the grid correctly. It should use snapped mouse position directly.

e.g.

- vertex position (1, 1)
- grid (10, 10)
- original mouse position (5, 5), we pressed mouse on (5,5)
- target mouse position (10, 10), we released mouse on (10, 10). Maybe real position is (9,9) or (11,11), it will be snapped to (10, 10) as grid configuration.
- The the distance is (10, 10) - (5, 5) = (5,5)
- Finally, vertex will move to (1, 1) + (5,5) = (6,6), which is not snapped on grid.
- We should let vertext move to (10, 10) directly.

summary: Loading a new scene in non-embedded subwindow causes game to freeze up on Wayland and Vulkan (Forward+)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in Godot v4.5.stable, v4.4.1.stable

Not tested in any other versions

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 19 Sep 2025 16:11:04 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (nvidia; 580.82.09) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When attempting to instantiate a scene within a subwindow, the game freezes a couple seconds after said window is open. 
Using --verbose to see what might have happened leads to nothing as no error gets displayed before it freezes. This seems to only occur when I am specifically using Wayland and Vulkan/Forward+ as switching back to X11, or switching to Compatibility, leads to the game working like normal again. Along with that, loading the game with embedded subwindows seems to cause no issue either and it runs like normal.

(Small Note: It may be related to a recent update I had performed on my system as just yesterday this wasn't a problem and scenes would load just fine. However as soon as I had done said update is when these problems occurred. I still wanted to make this bug report anyways just in case it isn't related to that update.)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d441078a-d0a9-4e31-a645-c36d4a91cf4c

The following video demonstrates exactly what is happening.

### Steps to reproduce

1: Make sure you are running a Wayland session and are using Vulkan/Forward+
2: Disable "Embed Subwindow" setting in Project Settings.
3: Create 2 scenes, with the main scene containing 2 windows. One visible window with a button and one invisible window with nothing in it. The other scene does not have to contain anything.
4: Make a script so that when the button is clicked, it will instance that second scene and add it to the currently invisible window 2, and afterwards make window 2 visible.
5: Press "Run Project"

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[WindowTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22579750/WindowTest.zip)

summary: Fix unicode input not emitting text_changed

description.text:
This fixes issue #110809 where when inserting a unicode character through either the menu or the keyboard alt method the `text_changed` signal is not emitted.

When a unicode character is inserted in either of these ways, the `_text_changed` function, which emits the signal, was never called. I inserted calls to `_text_changed` after a unicode character was inserted in the problematic ways. This issue only exists in `LineEdit` and not in `TextEdit`.

summary: Fix editor run bar not stopping when game view crashes

description.text:
When the game view crashes or is closed with the X button the editor run bar still showed the game running. In order to run the game (or scene) again the user needs to press stop before pressing run.

This change now correctly stops the editor run bar upon game view closing or crashing.

summary: Polygon 2D grid snap does not function consistently

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Single-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 7 265K (20 threads) - 31.37 GiB memory

### Issue description

With grid snapping enabled, the polygon 2d editor does not respect grid alignment. Creating or moving a vertex does not cleanly snap it to the grid point reliably. Clicking an existing vertex will cause it's alignment to shift around _near_ to the grid point, but can take many clicks to get it to be correctly on the point.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a polygon2d
2. In polygon editor, enable grid & grid snapping
3. Draw a set of vertexes. Observe their placement.
4. Move vertexes. Observer their placement.
5. Click on a vertex. Observe it's movement.
6. Repeat 5 multiple times, behavior repeats.

summary: `move_and_collide` causes crash when max number of Jolt bodies has been exceeded

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable
- Reproducible in: 4.5.stable
- Reproducible in: 4.6.dev [9283328fe70d27f148c403943a283d93a9785beb]

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.rc (928ee7023) - Fedora Linux 42 (Adams) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

When running [DOGWALK](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3775050/DOGWALK/) (Godot project available through the [Supporter Pack](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3833380/DOGWALK__Supporter_Pack/)) with Jolt Physics as the 3D physics engine, you pretty quickly run into a crash after the opening sequence, which seems to stem from `move_and_collide` being called on a body that has failed to allocate the underlying Jolt body, meaning its `jolt_body` pointer is null.

The issue of failing to allocate the `jolt_body` seems to stem from the default maximum number of Jolt bodies (10240) having been exceeded, which is already being emitted as an error, but the code in `JoltPhysicsDirectSpaceState3D::body_test_motion` fails to account for this scenario.

summary: Fix folder sorting in file dialogs for all sort options

description.text:
# PR Description: Directory Sorting Fixes and Improvements This PR addresses several long-standing issues related to directory sorting within Godot's file dialogs, ensuring more consistent and correct behavior. ### Key Changes: * **Fixes Directory Sorting Inconsistencies:** * Corrected the issue where `TYPE_REVERSE` sorting was not properly applied to directories. * Resolved the problem where `MODIFIED_TIME` sorting always defaulted to name-based sorting for directories. * Introduced proper, robust modified time-based sorting for directories. * Ensured that all reverse sort options (e.g., `TYPE_REVERSE`, `MODIFIED_TIME` with reverse) now function correctly for directories. * **Architectural Enhancements:** * Implemented a new `DirInfo` structure within `EditorFileDialog` to efficiently handle and store necessary information for time-based sorting. * Refactored and cleaned up the overall sorting logic, leading to improved code consistency and maintainability. ### Issues Addressed: Prior to this PR, users experienced the following problems when sorting folders in file dialogs: * When `Type (Descending)` sort was selected, folders were not correctly reversed in their order. * Even when `Modified Time` sort was chosen, folders would consistently be sorted by their name instead of their modification timestamp. * There was inconsistent sorting behavior observed between the main Editor file dialog and the Scene file dialog. ### Modified Files: * `scene/gui/file_dialog.cpp`: * Modified to always collect the `modified_time` for directories. * Adjusted reverse sorting conditions to ensure correct application. * `editor/gui/editor_file_dialog.cpp`: * Added the new `DirInfo` structure. * Implemented the core logic for time-based directory sorting using the new structure.
AS-IS TO-BE
* *Bugsquad edit, fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111036*

summary: Add auto-apply option for external scene changes

description.text:
The reason this option is needed is because when generating scenes via AI, pop-ups get in the way.

### Changes:
- Add new editor setting "interface/editor/auto_apply_external_scene_changes"
- Implement automatic reloading of externally modified scenes and project settings
- Skip dialog UI preparation when auto-apply is enabled
- Automatically reload scenes and project settings when changes are detected
- Maintain existing dialog-based workflow when auto-apply is disabled

This feature allows developers to automatically apply external changes to scenes without manual confirmation, improving workflow efficiency.

summary: Fix folder sorting in file dialogs for all sort options

description.text:
# PR Description: Directory Sorting Fixes and Improvements This PR addresses several long-standing issues related to directory sorting within Godot's file dialogs, ensuring more consistent and correct behavior. ### Key Changes: * **Fixes Directory Sorting Inconsistencies:** * Corrected the issue where `TYPE_REVERSE` sorting was not properly applied to directories. * Resolved the problem where `MODIFIED_TIME` sorting always defaulted to name-based sorting for directories. * Introduced proper, robust modified time-based sorting for directories. * Ensured that all reverse sort options (e.g., `TYPE_REVERSE`, `MODIFIED_TIME` with reverse) now function correctly for directories. * **Architectural Enhancements:** * Implemented a new `DirInfo` structure within `EditorFileDialog` to efficiently handle and store necessary information for time-based sorting. * Refactored and cleaned up the overall sorting logic, leading to improved code consistency and maintainability. ### Issues Addressed: Prior to this PR, users experienced the following problems when sorting folders in file dialogs: * When `Type (Descending)` sort was selected, folders were not correctly reversed in their order. * Even when `Modified Time` sort was chosen, folders would consistently be sorted by their name instead of their modification timestamp. * There was inconsistent sorting behavior observed between the main Editor file dialog and the Scene file dialog. ### Modified Files: * `scene/gui/file_dialog.cpp`: * Modified to always collect the `modified_time` for directories. * Adjusted reverse sorting conditions to ensure correct application. * `editor/gui/editor_file_dialog.cpp`: * Added the new `DirInfo` structure. * Implemented the core logic for time-based directory sorting using the new structure.
AS-IS TO-BE

summary: Fix region folding not loading properly

description.text:
Fixes #96370
Fixes #85780

ScriptTextEditor has a nice optimization™ that makes the script instances not fully loaded on startup. It loads the script, but does not initialize the CodeEdit until you switch tab, which makes it faster to restore scripts (similar to #110183).

The problem is that the editor also tried to restore script's state before it was enabled (i.e. initialized). So it went over folded lines and tried to fold them, but thanks to the aforementioned nice optimization™, it did not recognize that the folded lines are code regions (the language features were not fully loaded yet) and failed to fold them. Then the editor for whatever reason will immediately overwrite its stored state, so the folding is gone forever. It did work "sometimes", because the first script tab is initialized earlier for whatever reason.

This PR introduces `pending_state`. If editor tries to restore CodeEditor's state while it's not yet initialized, the state is stored in a variable and applied once the editor is fully initialized.

summary: Make Alt skip GUI tooltips and Shift show tooltips instantly

description.text:
Holding <kbd>Alt</kbd> skips GUI tooltips when hovering UI elements with the mouse, which is useful for large tooltips that can be intrusive.

Holding <kbd>Shift</kbd> makes tooltips show instantly instead of waiting for the usual tooltip delay (which defaults to 0.5 seconds). This is particularly useful on the Scene, FileSystem and Inspector docks.

I chose to use <kbd>Alt</kbd> instead of <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> as <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> is often used for multiple selection, so it's probably better not to interfere with tooltip behavior for that one.

Help is welcome on the TODO items listed below :slightly_smiling_face: 

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13267.

## Preview

*Not holding any modifier at first, then holding <kbd>Alt</kbd>, then holding <kbd>Shift</kbd>.*

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2bdb7b1d-b277-4dac-9092-493e8259309e

## TODO

- [ ] Implement in the inspector for property names. Right now, it only works on property values.
  - The code is [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f7630f1bfd9a7c65a5c144f85be38b4b64e3717/editor/doc/editor_help.cpp#L4608-L4609) and [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f7630f1bfd9a7c65a5c144f85be38b4b64e3717/editor/doc/editor_help.cpp#L4630-L4632), but I couldn't get it working without removing *both* `is_anything_pressed()` checks, which is probably not the right idea.
- [ ] Implement in the script editor's symbol hover tooltips.
  - The code is [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f7630f1bfd9a7c65a5c144f85be38b4b64e3717/scene/gui/code_edit.cpp#L462-L466), but I couldn't get it working (even though the branches were being reached when holding <kbd>Alt</kbd> or <kbd>Shift</kbd>). Something else is causing tooltips not to appear at all when *any* modifiers are pressed.

summary: Saving a GDScript file removes its executable permission (chmod +x)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Version: 4.5.stable [876b29033]

### System information

macOS Tahoe - M1 Mackbook Air

### Issue description

I followed the Command line tutorial in the Godot Docs.
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/editor/command_line_tutorial.html#running-a-script

I created a GDScript file and added executable permission to it using `chmod +x`.

After confirming that the script runs correctly from the CLI, I edited and saved the file in the Godot editor. 

When I tried to run the script again, I got a "permission denied" error.

It seems that saving the file in the editor removes the executable permission.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a GDScript and save it as `sayhello.gd`:
```gdscript
#!/usr/bin/env -S godot -s
extends SceneTree

func _init():
    print("Hello!")
    quit()
```

2. Make the file executable and run it:
```
$ chmod +x sayhello.gd
$ ./sayhello.gd
Hello!
```

3. Edit the file in the Godot editor and save it:
```gdscript
#!/usr/bin/env -S godot -s
extends SceneTree

func _init():
    print("Hello Again!")
    quit()
```

4. Try running it again:
```
$ ./sayhello.gd
zsh: permission denied: ./sayhello.gd
```


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Godot-permission-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22561539/Godot-permission-issue.zip)

summary: Add inline resource picker

description.text: This adds an inline quick load button to the script editor, like the color picker. This works for all ((triple-) single-quoted, (triple-) double-quoted) strings that start with `res://` or `uid://`. It's different from what was suggested in the discussion (only have it on `load`/`preload` calls), but I think this way it works for more use-cases like a custom load method. It has four modes: * Off * Only uid - only creates `uid://` paths * Only res - only creates `res://` paths * Preserve - respects whatever it is currently [Screencast_20250925_235258.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37c3832d-ef3f-4b2d-8bf9-15986773c065) This also adds inline hints for UID paths: While this isn't exactly like the chip mockup, I think it's more readable since you can see both the `res://` path and the original UID path. I refactored the inline object handlers from just being for the color picker to merge results from multiple sources, so maybe adding new inline objects will be easier in the future. I used a RegEx to locate the strings for easier development, I'm not sure if it's fine but I'm not sure it's easily possible to do it the same way the color picker does it. Resolves godotengine/godot-proposals/discussions/13192

summary: Autoload naming broken in 4.5

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: Godot Engine v4.5.stable.official - Not reproducible in: Godot v4.4.1 ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Vega 11 Graphics (RADV RAVEN) - AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics (8 threads) - 5.71 GiB memory ### Issue description Autoloads are not given a class name in PascalCase. in 4.4 and earlier versions, autoload script files would be written in `snake_case`, and automatically given a class name in `PascalCase`. now script files are no longer being renamed in autoload, leaving them in snake_case and forcing us to rename them, this causes both names to appear when typing until godot is restarted. behaviour in 4.5 (I renamed `combat_manager` to `CombatManager`, you have to look at `player`, which should be `Player`): how it's supposed to look like (4.4.1): giving files a name in `PascalCase` (MyScript.gd) would be against the gdscript style, as files must be in `snake_case` and class names in `PascalCase`: https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_styleguide.html#naming-conventions ### Steps to reproduce 1 - go to project settings->autoloads 2 - create a new script file, give it a name in snake_case (my_autoload.gd) 3 - create a new script on a node and try to access the autoload: `func _ready() -> void:` 4 - the suggestion will appear in snake_case [optional] 5 - rename it in PascalCase (MyAutoload) 6 - now both names are suggested (snake_case and PascalCase), until godot is restarted. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Tween stays valid after finishing until end of frame

description.text:
### Tested versions

-reproducible in v4.4.1 and v4.5

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7242) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.93 GiB memory

### Issue description

So when a tween is finished and has emitted its "finished" signal, it will still be "valid" until the end of the frame. This caused me an issue in the following way: I wanted to have an user action triggered by a click be executed after a tween is finished. Now to determine whether a tween was still ongoing or finished, I found the "is_valid" method on the Tween to be the closest match (more on that below). In case it was still ongoing I connect to the "finished" signal and do the action in a callback, otherwise I execute it immediately.

However if this user action happens on the same frame the tween finishes the following happens:
Begin Frame -> tween finishes and emits "finished" signal -> user event is handled -> tween is assumed to still be "valid" -> connect to "finished" signal -> End of Frame -> ("finished" signal is never emitted again we wait forever for action to happen)

In general we can split the frame the tween finishes on into two phases:
Begin Frame -> timespan A (tween valid and running) -> tween finishes (finished signal emitted) -> timespan B (tween valid but no longer running) -> End of Frame

As we can see this causes me to connect to the "finished" signal even though it will never be emitted again (although ... absolutely never? more on that later)

So first thing to address: why do I not use is_running() instead? Alright, so the thing I actually want to know is: "will this tween fire its 'finished' signal eventually or not? (assuming it's not killed pre-maturely)" and is_running() does not answer that question, because is_running() also returns false if the tween is paused (or stopped). And there is no way to tell if a tween is paused (would be too convenient, I guess). So the only other options are "is_valid" (which I thought would do what I wanted) or "custom_step(0)".

"custom_step ???" I hear you asking. Yes, because custom_step has this little bit in its doc: "Returns true if the Tween still has Tweeners that haven't finished." And that's great because that's pretty much what we want, it will return true even if the tween is paused and will return false once the tween is finished. Even for the half of a frame where it is still valid but actually already finished. So "custom_step(0)" is actually a workaround that does what we need, however it really is just misusing existing API.

Now it should be noted that "is_valid" is not incorrectly reporting the tween's status. The tween IS in fact still valid, you can still stop it, add new tweeners to it and restart it. And I think this behaviour is not desired, because this allows one to restart the tween again after it has finished, causing it to emit the "finished" signal twice (or multiple times). Expecting the "finished" signal of tweens (that are explicitly not meant to be re-used as godot docs make it abundandly clear) to only ever fire once and only once is a reasonable assumption to make, I think.

E.g. let's take the following scenario: Upon user action, I want to restart a tween from the beginning if it is still running. Let's say I also make use of "pause()" for my tweens, so I can't rely on "is_running". So I would check for "is_valid" to see if it is finished or not, and then call "stop()" and "play()" if it is still valid. And suddenly you have a frame-perfect bug in your game that would cause the "finished" signal to be executed a second time if you input on exactly the same frame the tween already did finish.

So what I really want is a method that will tell me whether "this tween will still fire its 'finished' signal eventually" (which I will create another issue for after this) and if "is_valid" is determined to be that, to make it so that having a tween finish and emit its "finished" signal, the tween will be in the same state immediately afterwards as if "kill" was called on it.
OR if it is determined that "is_valid" is not meant to be that, to put a big fat fucking warning in the docs to not rely on that (not a vague "A Tween **_might_** become invalid [...]", since a method that does not give me any guarantees about its behaviour is useless to me.), and tell me what to use instead. (which currently is "custom_step(0)" but again, that's just a workaround)


### Steps to reproduce

just attach the following script to a sprite, run the scene and wiggle your mouse for a second:
```
extends Node2D

var tween : Tween
var frameID : int = 0
var eventTracking : bool = false

func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	frameID += 1

func printTimestamp(msg:String):
	print(msg + " (FRAME:%d)" % frameID)

func _unhandled_input(_event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if tween == null:
		tween = create_tween()
		tween.tween_property(self, "rotation", PI * 2, 1).set_trans(Tween.TRANS_QUAD).set_ease(Tween.EASE_IN)
		tween.connect("finished", printTimestamp.bind("---> tween finished <---"))
		
		# the frame-perfect nature of this bug makes it hard to re-produce
		# just wiggle your mouse for a second and this will make sure that we are capturing debug output only around the time we are interested in
		var timer = get_tree().create_timer(0.97)
		timer.connect("timeout", self.set.bind("eventTracking", true))
		var timer2 = get_tree().create_timer(1.03)
		timer2.connect("timeout", self.set.bind("eventTracking", false))
	if eventTracking:
		printTimestamp("processing unhandled input")
		printTimestamp("tween valid: " + str(tween.is_valid()))
		printTimestamp("tween running: " + str(tween.is_running()))
		printTimestamp("tween custom step: " + str(tween.custom_step(0)))
```
just setting a timer is not enough since timers are processed before tweens in a frame, and we need to catch the time after tweens are processed and before the frame ends, which happens to be the case for _unhandled_input(). So to create an event exactly when the tween finishes (one second in) the user needs to wiggle their mouse (since MouseMotionEvents are the easiest to produce consistently). And two timers to make the debug output only be printed around the time the tween finishes. 

If done successfully you should see something like this in your output (actual frame numbers will vary):

```
[...]
processing unhandled input (FRAME:138)
tween valid: true (FRAME:138)
tween running: true (FRAME:138)
tween custom step: true (FRAME:138)
---> tween finished <--- (FRAME:139)
processing unhandled input (FRAME:139)
tween valid: true (FRAME:139)
tween running: false (FRAME:139)
tween custom step: false (FRAME:139)
processing unhandled input (FRAME:140)
tween valid: false (FRAME:140)
tween running: false (FRAME:140)
tween custom step: false (FRAME:140)
[...]
```

as one can see, the tween is valid and running until it is finished, after it is finished it is valid for one more frame (to be exact: until the end of the frame it finished in), and thus is_valid() returns true for one more time when called inside unhandled_input

And to note is, that the tween truly is still valid, one can stop it, add more tweeners and restart it at this point if one wanted to.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Example Project including a scene with the above mentioned script: [tweenissue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22540363/tweenissue.zip)

summary: Overriding `<AssemblyName>` in .csproj breaks C# autoloads/scene scripts (false “does not inherit from Node”)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: `Godot v4.5.stable.mono`
- Not reproducible in: `Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono`

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700G with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 31.79 GiB memory

### Issue description

**Brief**
Overriding the C# project’s assembly name using `<AssemblyName>` in the game’s `.csproj` causes **all C# autoloads and scene scripts** to fail to instantiate at startup. Godot reports that the script “does not inherit from `Node`”, even though it does. Removing the single `<AssemblyName>` line immediately restores normal behavior.

**Actual behavior (console/logs)**

```
Failed to instantiate an autoload, script 'res://v2/autoload/Game.cs' does not inherit from 'Node'.
C++ Error: Condition "!valid_type" is true. Continuing.
C++ Source: main/main.cpp:4335 @ start()
```

Editor also shows:

```
This inspector might be out of date. Please build the C# project.
```

**Expected**
Changing `<AssemblyName>` should not break type resolution for C# scripts; autoloads and scene nodes that inherit from `Node` should instantiate normally.

**Notes**

* Paths are correct (`res://v2/autoload/Game.cs`).
* Cleaning `.godot/mono`, `bin`, `obj`, rebuilding, and recreating the C# solution do not help while `<AssemblyName>` is present.
* Removing `<AssemblyName>` fixes the issue without further changes.

(See **Steps to reproduce** below for a minimal script and `.csproj` change.)


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new **Godot 4.5 (C#)** project.

2. Add `res://v2/autoload/Game.cs` with the following code and register it as **Autoload** (name: `Game`):

   ```csharp
   using Godot;

   namespace Repro.Autoload;
   public partial class Game : Node
   {
       public override void _Ready() => GD.Print("GAME AUTOLOAD OK");
   }
   ```

3. Run the project → it prints `GAME AUTOLOAD OK`.

4. Edit the project `.csproj` and **add `<AssemblyName>`**:

   ```xml
   <Project Sdk="Godot.NET.Sdk/4.5.0">
     <PropertyGroup>
       <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
       <AssemblyName>Custom.Assembly.Name</AssemblyName>
     </PropertyGroup>
   </Project>
   ```

5. From the project folder, rebuild:

   ```bash
   dotnet clean
   dotnet restore
   dotnet build -c Debug
   ```

6. Reopen Godot and run the project.

**Actual result:** startup fails; console shows:

```
Failed to instantiate an autoload, script 'res://v2/autoload/Game.cs' does not inherit from 'Node'.
```

**Expected result:** renaming the assembly should not break C# autoload/scene script instantiation.

**If attaching a minimal project:** open it in Godot, run once (works), then toggle the `<AssemblyName>` line in the `.csproj` as above, rebuild, and run again to reproduce.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

**Minimal repro**

1. Create a new Godot 4.5 C# project.
2. Add `res://v2/autoload/Game.cs` and register it as an Autoload:

   ```csharp
   using Godot;

   namespace Repro.Autoload;
   public partial class Game : Node
   {
       public override void _Ready() => GD.Print("GAME AUTOLOAD OK");
   }
   ```
3. Run the project → it works (`GAME AUTOLOAD OK`).
4. Edit the project’s `.csproj` and **add `<AssemblyName>`**:

   ```xml
   <Project Sdk="Godot.NET.Sdk/4.5.0">
     <PropertyGroup>
       <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
       <AssemblyName>Custom.Assembly.Name</AssemblyName>
     </PropertyGroup>
   </Project>
   ```
5. `dotnet clean && dotnet restore && dotnet build`, reopen Godot, run the project.

**Actual result**

* Autoload fails with the message above.
* Scene nodes with C# scripts also fail to instantiate with the same “does not inherit from Node” message.
* Editor often desynchronizes and keeps asking to rebuild the C# project.

summary: EditorUndoRedoManager.add_do_reference() corrupts node

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]

### System information

Windows 10 - v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] - Mobile Renderer

### Issue description

I wanted to create a little editor tool that which would replace Node3Ds selected in the scene with a scene selected in the FileSystem. This is the code for the button that has this functionality:

```gdscript
func _on_btn_replace() -> void:
	var nodes := EditorInterface.get_selection().get_selected_nodes()
	if not nodes: printerr("Nothing selected"); return
	
	for n1: Node in nodes:
		for n2: Node in nodes:
			if n1 == n2: continue
			if n1.is_ancestor_of(n2): printerr("Invalid selection"); return
	
	var path := EditorInterface.get_current_path()
	if not path or not (path.ends_with(".tscn") or path.ends_with(".scn")): printerr("No scene selected"); return
	
	var is_action := false
	var undo_redo := EditorInterface.get_editor_undo_redo()
	
	#print("Selected nodes: ", nodes)
	
	for n: Node in nodes:
		if not is_instance_valid(n) or n is not Node3D: continue
		
		var prefab = load(path)
		
		if not is_action:
			is_action = true
			undo_redo.create_action("Replace Selected Nodes", UndoRedo.MERGE_DISABLE, EditorInterface.get_edited_scene_root())
			undo_redo.add_do_method(EditorInterface.get_selection(), "clear")
			undo_redo.add_undo_method(EditorInterface.get_selection(), "clear")
		
		undo_redo.add_do_method(RatKingSimpleTools, "_replace", n, prefab)
		undo_redo.add_do_reference(n) # REMOVE THIS TO FIX BUG
		
		undo_redo.add_undo_method(RatKingSimpleTools, "_unreplace", n)
		undo_redo.add_undo_reference(n)
	
	if is_action:
		undo_redo.commit_action(true)
		#EditorInterface.mark_scene_as_unsaved()

static func _replace(node: Node3D, prefab: Variant) -> void:
	var p: Node3D = prefab.instantiate() as Node3D
	if not p: printerr("Wrong type for scene"); return
	node.get_parent().add_child(p, true)
	p.owner = node.owner
	p.transform = node.transform
	node.set_meta(&"__replacement", p)
	node.set_meta(&"__replacement_idx", node.get_index())
	node.get_parent().move_child(p, node.get_index())
	node.get_parent().remove_child(node)
	EditorInterface.get_selection().add_node(p)

static func _unreplace(node: Node3D) -> void:
	var p: Node = node.get_meta(&"__replacement")
	var i: int = node.get_meta(&"__replacement_idx")
	node.remove_meta(&"__replacement")
	node.remove_meta(&"__replacement_idx")
	p.get_parent().add_child(node)
	p.get_parent().move_child(node, i)
	node.owner = p.owner
	p.get_parent().remove_child(p)
	EditorInterface.get_selection().add_node(node)
```

I used the docs for EditorUndoRedoManager and UndoRedo to understand how to make my script undoable. So according to the docs, I have to use `add_do_reference()` and `add_unddo_reference()` to make sure the references to my generated and removed nodes aren't lost.

Now, when I do the following:
* replace a node with another instantiated scene
* undo the action
* manipulate the formerly replaced node, e.g. just moving around
then the formerly replaced nodes just vanishes, basically corrupting my scene.

I can fix this by removing the line `undo_redo.add_do_reference(n)` - no corruption happens then. The question is why though. And if this is expected behaviour.

### Steps to reproduce

* open the provided project and open the main_scene scene.
* click on the cubes in the scene
* click on the sphere.tscn scene in the FileSystem
* click on the "Replace Selected with Scene" button in the custom editor in the bottom right
* undo the action, via Ctrl+Z
* manipulate the formerly replaced node(s), e.g. just move them around

The node(s) should vanish, which is the issue.

[test-undoredo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22541096/test-undoredo.zip)

summary: Godot 4.5 crashes on reimport_files after a timer timeout inside settings_changed

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.rc.custom_build [876b29033]
v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc (876b29033) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7703) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600H with Radeon Graphics (12 threads) - 15.34 GiB memory

### Issue description

I'm trying to synchronize my `EditorPlugin` theme with the editor theme but instead I get crashes. This is a simplified version of my plugin's code (from MRP):
```gdscript
func _enter_tree() -> void:
	var settings := EditorInterface.get_editor_settings()
	settings.settings_changed.connect(func ():
		if settings.check_changed_settings_in_group("interface/theme"):
			print("theme changed")
			await get_tree().create_timer(2.0).timeout
			EditorInterface.get_resource_filesystem().reimport_files(["res://icon.svg"])
			print("after reimport")
	)
```
When you change editor's theme and then close the settings window, after ~2 seconds the timer will fire and Godot will try to reimport specified resources and will crash (or print an error) after that with different reasons (sometimes it crashes in `Memory::free_static`, sometimes in `SceneTreeTimer::is_process_in_physics`).

The crash doesn't happen every time but it's does happen pretty often. It usually always crashes if you open a project, go straight to the settings, change editor's theme and close the settings window. The import dialog says "executing post-import operations" and then the editor crashes without any crash log.

The reason for the reimport is that I have icons that I show in the editor. The icons are marked with `editor/convert_colors_with_editor_theme` and `editor/scale_with_editor_scale`. But the color of the icons is set for the initial editor's theme and the sequential changes to the theme do not change the color of the icons.

The reason for the timer (which is simplified a bit in the MRP) is that when `settings_changed` fires the theme is not applied yet and when you call `EditorInterface.get_editor_theme()` it still returns the old theme and only after some time (and a small freeze) it changes to an actual value.

So considering all of the above I am trying to reimport the icons (and do some other stuff) after the editor's theme has changed and has applied to the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

Make an `EditorPlugin` and put this code inside:
```gdscript
func _enter_tree() -> void:
	var settings := EditorInterface.get_editor_settings()
	settings.settings_changed.connect(func ():
		if settings.check_changed_settings_in_group("interface/theme"):
			print("theme changed")
			await get_tree().create_timer(2.0).timeout
			EditorInterface.get_resource_filesystem().reimport_files(["res://icon.svg"])
			print("after reimport")
	)
```
Then re-enable it to load the code. Open editor settings, change editor's theme and immideately close the editor. Wait for the timer to fire.
You'll get either
```
ERROR: Condition "p_I->data != this" is true. Returning: false
   at: List<class Ref<class SceneTreeTimer>,class DefaultAllocator>::_Data::erase (C:\Code\godot-src\core/templates/list.h:230)
```
or a crash.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[settings_changed_timer_crash_2025-09-25_14-44-00.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22535761/settings_changed_timer_crash_2025-09-25_14-44-00.zip)

summary: Disable checkbox for plugins with no script

description.text:
If no script is defined for a plugin, the checkbox won't do anything anyways. This makes it so that if the script name is empty in ```plugin.cfg```, the checkbox isn't added. The editor itself does not allow you to _not_ define a script, but I'm not sure that the in-editor dialog is intended for advanced usage such as this.

This essentially allows any plugin to show information without confusing users with the checkbox. For example, the git plugin used to show information in the menu but it was removed because users were confused about the checkbox ([#267](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-git-plugin/issues/267)). With this pr the information could be added back and the issue would still be solved.

summary: Fix Metal toolchain detection on macOS/iOS in order for metallib to be properly generated with Shader Baker

description.text:
### Problem

Exporting projects with **Shader Packer** enabled failed to generate metalib on macOS/iOS.  

This was caused by issues in how the shell command to query the `metal` toolchain was constructed:

- The exec command string was malformed, so the query always failed.  
- Minimum OS version flags (`-mmacosx-version-min`, `-mios-version-min`) were appended in the wrong position, causing them to be ignored.  
- The exit code from `metal` was not validated correctly.  

As a result metalib was not generated,  leading to very long first-load times (e.g. ~60s in the TPS Demo) when no shader cache was present.  

---

### Fix

- Correctly construct the `metal` toolchain query command.  
- Place min-version flags in the proper position so they are respected.  
- Check and propagate the exit code from `metal`.  

With this fix, shaders are baked as intended, significantly reducing startup times as opposed to only SPIRV versions othrewise.

---

Fixes: #110870

summary: [macOS] Inspector: A property's tooltip appears then disappears

description.text:
### Tested versions

- found in: v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]
- reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], 
- not reproducible in: v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Tahoe (26.0.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M4 Max - Apple M4 Max (16 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

When hovering over inspector property names (Position, Rotation, etc.), the tooltip for the property appears then immediately disappears even though the mouse has not moved. Seems to happen randomly; I cannot find reasoning why it sometimes happens and sometimes does not. I tried selecting the Inspector window, de-selecting it, etc. It happens both if using and if not using Stage Manager.

### Steps to reproduce

- Select a node
- hover mouse over a property in the Inspector pane

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[4.5-bug-report-3.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22522354/4.5-bug-report-3.zip)

summary: Shader Baker failing to compile .metallib on macOS: Unable to determine toolchain properties to compile .metallib

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5 stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Tahoe (26.0.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads) - 48.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Godot Editor on macOS fails to export with compiled .metallib due to an error:

**_Unable to determine toolchain properties to compile .metallib_**

Without .metallib the exported macOS builds (and iOS) are extremely slow to load the first time. Tps DEMO loads in 60 seconds the first time on a 16" MacBook M3 MAX.

Please note that I have everything setup locally, as tested. 


### Steps to reproduce

Try to export any project using shaders, such as [TPS DEMO](https://github.com/godotengine/tps-demo ) from Godot.

With Shader Packer enabled on the export settings. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any Godot project which uses shaders, such as TPS.

https://github.com/godotengine/tps-demo

summary: Engine and system freeze after stopping to play a project

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5, 4.4, 4.3, 4.2, 4.1 stable editions


### System information

void linux x86-64 glibc xfce edition (all drivers are up to date)

### Issue description

The engine freezes after i stop playing a project(press pause/stop button or close the window with a project). Right after i press these buttons godot starts not to response any actions, after about 10 seconds the whole system freezes. (nothing printed in the console). After the pressing it becomes impossible to kill any godot's thread. The issue is with both 2d and 3d "modes", with any drawer.

### Steps to reproduce

Just create a scene in a project, launch it and stop by pressing stop/pause/restart button or closing a window with the project playing

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Litteraly any project

summary: Add solo/hide/lock/delete buttons to node groups in bezier track editor

description.text:
Resolves godotengine/godot-proposals#11340.

Co-authored with @Arnklit.

This adds four new buttons to the node groups found in the bezier animation track editor: solo, hide/show, lock/unlock and delete. This is basically the same as what's already found on each of the individual bezier tracks.

Here's a short clip showing what it looks like and how they behave.

[BezierNodeGroupButtons.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/debaca4b-417e-4826-a3e2-6b06da1a41a9)

Note that no per-node state has been introduced to accommodate for things like visibility or locking, so you're only ever toggling the state of the individual tracks themselves.

Unlike the proposal linked above, which mentioned that a solo button might not make sense for node groups, we felt that it did in fact make sense and could be useful, so we added that as well.

---

Also note that there are a couple of pre-existing issues surrounding all of this that this PR does not do anything to resolve:

1. When deleting all tracks, and there are regular value tracks in the animation, you will see an error about an out-of-bounds index.
2. When deleting tracks prior to the selected track, the selected track will shift.
3. When undoing deletions and there are hidden/locked tracks following the newly inserted tracks, those hidden/locked states will shift to other tracks.

We did however throw in a fix for an out-of-bounds error when deleting all bezier tracks, but the others we felt would complicate this already hefty PR too much.

We did also take the opportunity to change/fix the alignment/margins of the icons, which seemed a bit off.
